Meta Data v9

Event Name/Title

How To Be Krishna Conscious

Event Date

21 Jul 1989

Event Location

India, Mumbai,Iskcon Chowpatty

Event Type

Temple - SB class

Transcriber

Anuradha Tripathi

Completed

12 Apr 2018

Tags – Places

Radhagopinath temple,

Haridas Thakur, Jesus and prostitute,

Tags – Themes

Lecture Transcription

Why is mind lamenting?

[Devotee:] Asking question [sound not clear] he was creating problems for me in my spiritual life, and I realized and so our friendship broke off, [not clear] very good for me spiritually, but my mind was not willing to accept it. So I started realizing it wasn’t really very good for me. So why is my mind lamenting. I don’t understand, and I don’t understand, what should be the solution?

[Maharaj:] That is why Krishna in the third chapter of Gita [not clear0.39] lower self with the higher self, a lower self is the desires of the senses, as well as the desires of the mind. Some of our emotional… emotional attachments are based on our minds, since gratification, the higher- intelligence is given by God. The difference between a human being and an animal, is that man is called a rational animal, that means, we have an intelligence by which we can discriminate, and that, when that discrimination is based on the word of God, that is real intelligence, that is a higher self. Higher self is the soul. The lower self is the body and the mind, the senses and the mind. Ah, so when our activities are judged, very discreetly on the basis of that higher intelligence given by God, that is Krishna consciousness. So sometimes, senses and the mind are very attached to certain people, and certain things of this world, that are not favorable for our, against our spiritual life, those things must be rejected. Rupa Goswami explains, to make advancement in Krishna consciousness is very simple; ‘you accept those things favorable for spiritual life, and reject those things unfavorable for spiritual life.’ [audio breaks] so we have to understand, what is good for me, and what is not good for me, on that basis. Now, the fact that we lament over something, that we are conditioned to enjoy, when we give it up, is natural. Krishna says in Gita, ‘tatra tiṣṭhāmi nārada’ he says that, “The non permanent appearance of happiness and distress, and its disappearance in due course are like the appearance and disappearance of the winter and summer seasons, one must learn to tolerate them without being disturbed.” So these attachments that we have in this world, we have to learn to tolerate the happiness and the lamentation, the pleasure and the pain, the honor and the dishonor, ah. So considered it is nothing bad, it is a natural thing. With our intelligence, we have to take proper action, and we have to tolerate these feelings of lamentation, and we have to learn to transcend them, by utilizing our intelligence to fix our consciousness on the higher principles of life, the higher goal of life. Krishna tells Arjuna in Gita,

aśocyān anvaśocas tvaṁ
prajṣā-vādāṁś ca bhāṣase
gatāsūn agatāsūṁś ca
nānuśocanti paṇḍitāḥ

[Bg. 2.11]

[The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: While speaking learned words, you are mourning for what is not worthy of grief. Those who are wise lament neither for the living nor for the dead.]

The wise man does not lament for the living or the dead. So this lamentation is due to previous material conditioning, so we have to purify ourselves from these material conditions but through the process of purification while those, anarthas, those unwanted desires are still in our heart, we have to simply tolerate them. Just like if, you're sick, the doctor may give you some treatment, which you don't like, but it's good for you. So you with your intelligence, you know, it's good for you, so you do it, and at the same time you tolerate the inconvenience for a higher goal. If you're not willing to tolerate it for a higher goal, you're not worthy of that higher goal, ah. There's always inconvenience, to do something, to attain something great there will always be inconvenience. We have to tolerate whatever inconvenience, and push forward for that goal.

Use everything in the Krishna’s service

[Devotee:] Some of the scientist wonderful discoveries they have made, [sound][not clear5.13] the time [sound] [not clear] these discoveries inventions [sound] [sound] [sound not clear] so we are taking advantage of those discoveries and inventions, [sound not clear5.27] for example car, car is [not clear 5.32] if we just stop the progress and involve in chanting the name of the Lord, or indulge in [not clear5.47]

[Maharaj:] Krishna's says in Bhagavad-Gita that, he is the intelligence of the intelligence. He is the ability in man. Do you think man created the car? It was only by the intelligence given by God, that man has any capacity to create anything, ah. God is a supreme creator. So ultimately the credit goes to Krishna. You don't even have a brain to think, or hands to move without the grace of God. That is true. So second of all, because you are a devotee, it doesn't mean that you're not productive in contributing to society, you're the most productive in contributing to society, but you have the proper wisdom of what society needs, so that you'll produce the right things, with the right motives. So let's take automobile for instance, if in a God conscious society, there is truly a need, and a benefit for automobiles, devotees, will God not give the intelligence to devotees, he can give the intelligence to a materialist, certainly give the intelligence to his devotee, huh, but he'll use it for the right motive. Now, the question is debatable, whether the invention of the car was good or bad? We see, the car itself, if it's used in Krishna's service, since it's already made, it's already there, if it's used in Krishna's service, it's good. If it's used for the purpose of sinful activities, or materialistic activities, it's bad, from the spiritual point of view. So it's already there. So therefore, that, we have nothing against anything, that's already here, as long as it's used for the proper motivation. Now we can trace back, was it a good idea initially, to make the car, ah? That's debatable. Has it created more problems or done more benefit? It used to be here in India, that everyone would work within a few minutes, walk from where they lived. And everything was, literally, this is the way India was. Everything was provided, right in your own village. It was a simple life. A quiet life, absolutely pure water, absolutely pure air, very pure consciousness, greediness and …and thievery was not, because everybody basically had what they needed, there was plenty of food, and you and everybody and anything you wanted, there was somebody in your little area that could provide it. There was the doctors, who didn't need lawyers in those days [devotees laugh] so it was a simple little, it was a simple life, very peaceful, where people could naturally love each other, they could, the families were close together, and there was a very high spiritual purpose, in the mornings the whole village would come together, this is the way India was, and and, have kirtans, and in the evenings the whole village would come together, and discuss Ramayana, Mahabharata, take rest in a simple nice little house, and… and…and be happy. That's the, now… now has the invention of the automobile, than good or bad. It was an artificial. It was a creation of an artificial necessity. Now in Bombay, the pollution is, you're smoking like one to two packs of cigarettes, it's just breathing in this city, which means, it's killing you, were just breathing in this city, is killing your body, plus traffic jams, just to get to work or anywhere else, or to visit a friend, everything's so spread out, you have to be you know through… through hours of traffic, and people honking horns, of people crashing into each other, people getting angry at each other, and annoyed and swearing at each other, huh, plus there's automobile accidents, people are getting killed, ah. So while it's already here, and the society is…is…is created in this way, hope to make the best use of a bad bargain, we'll take whatever is here, and use it for Krishna's service. But the question is, ideally, is this really the best thing for… for…for happiness, peace, prosperity in…in human society, to have all these highly technological achievements, or are they simply creating pollution, greed, disturbance, and more inconvenience than convenience?

[Devotee:] [not clear10.19] let’s take for example antibiotics, they discovering new…new bacteria,[not clear10.28]

[Maharaj:] Well, that may be, that is true, that under the conditions of the artificial situation in society today, in order to combat the reactions, we have to make artificial cures, but of course, a natural society a natural peaceful society, that would probably be much less of these sorts of unnatural diseases by which you have to create all these antibiotics, and so forth, right? I mean most of the diseases we're talking about our due to pollution, and unnatural living. [Music]

[Devotee:] So all the progression we are making is taking us away from spiritual life, material life, [not clear11.18]

[Maharaj:] All that you have to do, is look at the progression of the human society, over the years is it coming closer to God, or going farther from God, are people becoming more religious, or more materially minded. So that's a sign of the… the direction society is going to, and all the material achievements, making big… big plans for happiness through material arrangements, and in the course, they are forgetting the real needs of the soul, and the natural devotion of God that is inherent within them.

It is sinful to kill

[Devotee:] I had problems a few months back, there is a patient, she was two months pregnant, and she was having a disease, which can have congenital anomalies, the physician was not prepared to terminate the pregnancy. There were chances of the child taking birth with the anomalies like short lives, or any possible way brain defects and so, he was prepared and he was [not clear 12.38] what is my role now, how can I tell you,[not clear]

[Maharaj:]The only time, it's justified to kill, as if it is if you're defending your own life. You don't have to kill in all circumstances, but it is justified, it is not sinful to kill, if it's defense. So this is a scriptural injunction. So therefore, if the woman, if her life is seriously and honestly being threatened, by this child, then she has a right to kill it, to defend herself, but for any other purpose it’s murder, first-degree in the eyes of God [devotees speaks] If it's a question of how the child comes out, you have no right, anymore, than somebody, who's retarded on the streets you have no right to kill it, right? If somebody's deformed on the street, do you have the right to kill it, just because it's deformed? That child has the right to live, whether it's inside of the room, or outside of the room.

[Devotee:] If suppose the disease is not killing him, [not clear 14.2]

[Maharaj:] By the laws of God, it is sinful.

[Devotee:] Generally if the mother is in the continuous pregnancy, her life is in danger, if she is continuously pregnant, so she has the right continuous to abort…

[Maharaj:] if it's seriously and honestly analyzed, and then it's not just very whimsically that if my life is in danger, so let me kill this child, out of convenience…

[Devotee:] [not clear] then she may die…

[Maharaj:] if… if the doctor, he has to be honest and he's sitting before God, when he makes this decision. Is it an emergency, life-or-death situation for the woman, if it's not, then he may be implicating himself in a very sinful act…. [Devotee:] Maharaj, Maharaj,[not clear 14.55] is asking,[not clear] where Krishna says, if you remember me at the time of death, surely you will come to me,[not clear 15.7] and say Hare Krishna, then you will go back to God.

[Maharaj:] You will not be able to think of Krishna, thinking of Krishna, is not based on your concoction, if you are Krishna conscious, and pure in your life, Krishna will reveal himself, as the last thought, at the time of death. The fact is if a person were to do that, he would not be able to think of Krishna, at the time of death.

Dedicate your life in the service of God

[Devotee:] [not clear 15.43] you must do this and say Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna… and cheat yourself, so at that time we are thinking of Krishna and Krishna and go back to Krishna.

[Maharaj:] If you cannot think of Krishna, you can only think of Krishna, when he reveals himself, you can say Hare Krishna, but Krishna will not appear in his name, nor will he appear as a thought, if you're doing it flagrantly, huh. The only way you can think of Krishna, at the time of death is, if you prepare your life in devotion, then he will reveal himself, even if you're in an unconscious coma, if you have dedicated your life in the service of God, he will appear before you, at the time of death, as the last thought. At the time of death, you have no control, what you're gonna think, you may say Hare Krishna, and then you may pull the trigger, but that, between that Hare Krishna, and then you pull the trigger, there's an interval of, what you think of, at the time of death. Krishna is not going to reveal himself to you, to such a criminal mind person. He's going to reveal himself to someone who has practiced with great integrity, during his life, to be his devotee. At the time of death, is such a traumatic experience, you cannot just choose at that time, what you want to think of. It's based on, what you've done in your life that Krishna reveals, what you're most attached to in life, is what you think of it and death. If you're attached to atrocities, throughout your life, at the time of death, you're gonna have an atrocious thought. If you're attached to a woman, more… more than anything else at the time of your life, at the time of death, you're going to think of that woman. If you're attached, if you cultivate attachment to Krishna, more than anything else during your life, then at the time of death, Krishna is going to reveal himself to you, as your last thought. That last thought, is not based on a deliberate circumstantial decision, at the instance, that thought is determined, by the overall consciousness, that you have cultivated throughout your life, ah, that appears, by the grace of God, at the time of death. In other words, that last thought is a flash of your life, the flash of your over all life's consciousness just appears as a flash to you, at the time of death, and that's what you think. It's just like a final exam. Do you think you could just do all nonsense, and at the time of the final exam, you're gonna get the best grades, you're not gonna be able to remember, unless you study hard to prepare for it, right? Death is the final exam, according to you, how you have prepared during your life, you're gonna be prepared, to think of Krishna at death. It's not at the last minute, you're just gonna be able to think, well, I'll do all nonsense, and all I have to do is, just get good grades, at the final exam season. [laughter] But can you get good grades unless you prepare, throughout the whole semester? Impossible! In college you may be able to cheat, but when it comes to the time of death, there's no… there's nobody… there's nobody's paper to look over at, you cannot cheat it, you cannot cheat God. So now is the time to start preparing. Don't think you can cheat Krishna, at the time of death.

[Devotee:] Why is this human sufferings are, at the selective places in the world some places may [not clear 19.44] these people have some disease like pandemic there, why is it like that, or the way like Americans, they are, they are good looking, they are strong, they are rich, why is it they are rich and Indians, they are like this?

[Maharaj:] [laughter]

[Devotee:] [not clear20.25]

Realize your spiritual nature

[Maharaj:] Ha, that question could be answered in many ways, one is the records that they set, the animals could do better. Do you think the fastest runner in America, can run as fast as a cheetah, or as a, or a tiger, or even a dog? Do you think the most powerful weightlifter in America, could lift as much as an elephant, what are the records? Do you think the best gymnastic in America, could do what a monkey could do? No, not possible. They don't even compete. [Devotee:][not clear 21.15]

[Maharaj:]No, so what we are talking about, what the animals could do, human life is not meant to…to, to do what the animals could do better. When it comes to sex life, a pigeon can have more sex, than you can ever imagine having in one day. When it comes to gymnastics, of you know a wheeling around, a monkey could do more with his tail, than you can do through, every limb of your body, right? When it comes to playing football, if you put a gorilla in the field, I mean everybody's gonna run away, in total fear, right? Wrestlers, boxers, you think they could fight against a gorilla, for a tiger, or even a little Cobra, [laughter] put Mike Tyson in the ring with a cobra, or with a scorpion, or with a tiger, or a lion, practically…practically of… there’s hundreds of species of life, that can utterly destroy him within seconds in the ring, right? So is this human civilization? The animals are better at…at…at these activities of defending and athletics. So that's not advancement of human society. Advancement of human society is based on the principle of…of…of realizing your spiritual nature. As far as prosperity, the reason why India's suffering is because, India has been given the most, but it's doing the least, for one who much is given much is expected, just like you have a child, how old is?

Live according to the heritage of the spiritual nation

[Devotee:] Three years old,

[Maharaj:] three years old? So if he… if he does naughty things, do you take him very seriously, you give him a little punishment, but when he's sixteen and he does the same things, what do you do? You punish him very severely, right? Because he knows better, just this lake this child may slap his mother on the face, when he's mad, right? Child might be mad at the mother, endoscope. So what does the mother do? She just gives him a little slap in the face and says, “this is nonsense don't do this.” Now, what happens if the sixteen year old child punches the mother on the face, it's taken more seriously, he's going to be punished more severely, because he knows better, so similarly, spiritually, the people of the West, know practically nothing, they have a very… very limited understanding of…of…of the purpose of life and spirituality, the Scriptures give a very… very modified conception of God, and the goal of life. Whereas India, for millions and millions of years, India as its heritage, have had the greatest sages, the greatest sadhus, the [not clear] the highest scriptures, the most complete information of God consciousness, a history of such great saints. India has the greatest responsibility in the world to follow the laws of God. When India starts chasing after the things of the West, the materialistic ways, and leaving behind, its own heritage, engages in sinful activities. India is going to suffer hundreds of times worse than, if somebody from any other place in the world does same thing, because there's so much, by your birth in India, you are responsible to live according to the, according to the heritage of the spiritual nation, ah. And if you don't, you're going to be smashed by the laws of nature more than anybody else. That is why, there's so much poverty. That is why, there is so much disease. For much, one who much is given, much is expected.

America is going to fall apart

[Devotee:] But what was the reason of America’s prosperity [not clear 25.27]

[Maharaj:] But what, this prosperity is such an illusion. How long do you think it's going to last? One, two hundred years, it’s only two hundred years old, America. We’ll talk India's civilization, is goes back to millions and millions of years. America's only two hundred years old. The persons who came to America, were religious people, the founding fathers of America, they left Europe, for religious freedom. They were very, by their… by their standard of religion, which is of course, much lower than the Indian standard, the vedic standard, they were very pious religious people, and by their piety, they raped good karma, ah. So they're living, their attaining now the fruits of their good karma, their piety, the religiosity, but, because now they're engaging in such sinful activities, we find the whole entire system of America is falling to pieces. I mean anyone who has an open mind, who lives there, sees, that the whole country is coming apart, by it seems. It sound the downfall, it cannot survive much longer. There's hate, there's riots. The economy is so shaky. There a huge national debt. The government is taxing taxing taxing more and more, the people are becoming frustrated. The pollution is becoming horrible. Diseases are just plaguing the society, ah. You'll see, within your lifetime perhaps, America’s gonna, America’s gonna fall apart, and if not very soon, after. Historically every great empire, that's built up, when they were simple persecuted people, striving pious religious, they build up, then they become very comfortable, they become very prosperous, and they become very lazy, very sinful, very decadent, they don't need God anymore, and then the decline. Homosexuality starts becoming prominent. Lack of trust, in the leadership becomes prominent. If you read historically, the fall of the Roman Empire, the form of the fall of the Greek Empire, it…it…it systematically all takes place step by step, the same way, America is following all those steps exactly, it's in its downfall, cannot survive. So it's just the temporary result to their pious lives, but soon they're going to be getting the temporary results for all the sins they're performing now, which is gonna be a tremendous smashing, rashing, but not as much as India. Because India knows better, the Americas hardly know better, ah.

Sex according to religious principle

[Devotee:] Maharaj, what’s your idea about the [not clear 28.44] same sex partners,

[Maharaj:] The ideal is, for the purpose of service to God. In Bhagavad Gita Krishna says, “Sex according to religious principles, I am that sex.” Service to God would mean that, a man and a woman come together for the purpose of procreating children in this world is to raise them in spiritual consciousness. That is the ideal, to have sex for that purpose.[mike sound] That sex life is yoga, that sex life is a spiritual activity, if it's done with that motivation, for that purpose. And of course, there are samskaara’s or purificatory… preparations, that one undergoes before having sex life, for that purpose. Garbhadan, samskaaras, which means according to the consciousness of the male and female, at the time of sex life, that will determine the type of spirit soul, that enters into the womb. So therefore, the male and female want to invite a very pious and religious person into this world, to help the world. Varna-sankara means unwanted population, when sex when… when children are conceived, simply out of lust, a very low-class, very sinful natured person will take shelter of the womb, in most cases, in those circumstances, but when the, for instance, many parents they chant fifty… fifty malas of the Hare Krishna mantra, the man and the woman, live a very… very and perform many more other purificatory activities, before having sex. In this way, they are sp Krishna conscious, they are so pure in their consciousness, that they're going to attract a very… very elevated soul, to take shelter in their womb, and then they're going to raise that child in such a way, with the responsibility to God, that the child becomes peaceful, happy and Krishna consciousness in its life.

Engage in the positive spiritual activities

[Devotee:] The desire, cannot just vanish like that,

[Maharaj:] But if you are engaging in positive spiritual activities, you are engaging in positive spiritual activities then these desires are replaced, with higher more virtuous desires. Spiritual life means to purify our desires, by pew… by spiritual activities and spiritual thoughts. It is true, that you cannot just give up desires. You cannot just give up your sinful desires, but you can purify them, by chanting the names of God, by reading the scriptures, by engaging in devotional activities. This purifies our consciousness, and these lower desires can no longer remain. Can darkness remain in the presence of the sun? Sinful desires are due to ignorance, but Krishna’s light, the presence of the light of Krishna, the sinful desires which are born of ignorance, cannot remain within our heart. So the main thing, we have to concentrate on is feeling our mind, filling our consciousness filling our senses, with the light of Krishna and then it will dispel all the darkness of these sinful desires that are lurking within our mind, ah. So this is the ideal in marriage life, according to the principles of Bhagavad-Gita. Now one may not be able to follow this ideal on the highest level immediately, but one should be striving in that direction, and gradually through purification of the heart, through devotional activities, one will be able to come to that standard. Prahlada Maharaja in Srimad-Bhagavatam describes that, sex life for a materialist is nothing more than scratching an itch, it has no more significance than that, you're just itching like crazy and it's not enjoyment, it's just it's actually relief of anxiety and that relief of anxiety is considered by a foolish person to be pleasure, [laughter] it's just like if you have poison ivy, or poison oak, some horrible rash, and it's itching like crazy, and it's painful, it's suffering, and when you scratch it, it's like ah… Oh, it's so nice, right? But then what happens when you stop scratching it. It is just more than ever, because you've irritated it. What do you tell a patient who has an itching rash? Do you tell them scratch it, you will feel good, you tell them tolerate it, and it'll go away. The more you scratch it, the more it’s irritated. Well that's what sex life is all about, it's an itch, and it itches so bad that you just have to scratch it, and while you're scratching and it's just like ah… so nice, but then after you scratched it, what you've done is just irritated that itch so bad that next time it comes around it's gonna it's gonna scratchy, it's… it's even worse, but then if you tolerate the itch, and take the proper medication which is the holy name of God, then gradually the itch will go away, huh. [laughter]

Have children and make them Krishna Conscious

[Devotee:] But in the Srimad-Bhagavatam is that, [not clear 34.29] hundreds of children…

[Maharaj:]… There if they are God-conscious, they are not simply scratching itches, they are propagating the world with God conscious children. There's a need for God conscious children. The duty of a grihasta, is to have God conscious children, that means you perform the proper samskaras, you have the child in the right consciousness, in pure consciousness, and you invite very… very elevated souls, and then you raise them in God consciousness. You can have hundreds of children if you're… if you're in that consciousness and you're able to maintain, and protect them all spiritually. Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati Thakura our guru Maharaj's guru, param guru, he used to say that, “If I could make, if I could raise children in this world, and make them Krishna conscious, I would have hundreds of children.” But he decided to take sanyas, [laughter] because he thought it was very difficult to do it. But this is the duty of a grihasta, however many children you can maintain, and protect very very nicely in such a way that they can become Krishna conscious, you have that many children, if you have too many, you will not be able to raise them properly, ah. But it's the duty of the grihasta to help human society, by bringing in good population. It's a service to God and it's a service to humanity,

You have no right to kill

[Devotee:] [not clear 36.8] a material man will never have any idea about the birth…

[Maharaj:] But you have to understand, that that child has taken birth in your womb, according to your karma. It's not the child, it's not the child’s, it's the child's fault, that he has that karma of taking that body, and it's your fault by your karma, do you have it, as a son, right? You are a doctor, now you have no right to kill it, any more than outside of the womb. What if a have, a child, somebody has a child, and the child gets in some accident, and is lame, blind, deaf and dumb for life. Do you have a right to kill it? You may not like it as a child anymore, might be completely inconvenient and embarrassing for you. But do you have a right to kill it? No more outside of the womb, than inside of the womb. This whole concept of pro-choice is so selfish, all the choices are for the mother, but they give no choice to the child. The child is in the most innocent condition, imaginable, and nobody gives it. Does anyone ask the child, can we abort you? How many children you think would raise their hands and say, “Yes, kill me” absolutely none. So yes, a woman has the freedom to make choice, but not when it's at the cost of the life of another human being, and a doctor is in the same position, and at the end the doctor, the woman, everyone involved is hundred percent responsible, for the actions, they take. Human life is a life of responsibility, killing a child in the womb according to karma is has no, it is not different than killing a child outside of the womb. Whether it's living inside or living outside, it's living, it's growing. It has as much right to live inside as outside. It's like if you kill someone when they're sleeping, is it less of offence than to kill someone when they're awake. You may say, “Oh, well he was not conscious, when I killed it,” right? Doesn't matter! So the living being… yes?

[Devotee:] [not clear38.44] that if the child is in danger, or mother is in danger, so [not clear] is very rare situation,…

[Maharaj:] We already discussed this, and the answer is, according to scripture, it's sinful to kill any living being, unless your life is being threatened. To defend your own life, you have a right to kill. You don't have to kill, but you do have that right, if that child taking womb, birth, in your womb, is threatening the mother's life, she has a right to kill it, if it's not for any other mode, if its first-degree murder in the eyes of God. Now anybody can just say, well, it's endangering my life, because after all I have high blood pressure, so, [laughter] you have to face, you have to sit before God, in your own mind, and you have to know is this an emergency life-or-death predicament, if it is not, you have no right to take its life, only if it's a serious life-and-death situation, do you have any right in the eyes of God of taking the life of that child. It cannot be a maybe this, or maybe that situation, it has to be definite, specific life-or-death. The doctor's karmically has…has the same responsibility as the mother. If the doctor is implicated by his decision, or by his operation, in killing a child specifically and exclusively, for the emergency protection of the woman's life, the doctor has no karma, but if it's for any other purpose, and if God doesn't see it, the same way you do, [laughter] that the doctor, has pulled the trigger, and he has performed first-degree murder, in the eyes of God, and according to the laws of karma.

Why should I take my mother’s karma?

[Devotee:] Maharaj, my mother performed [not clear 41] I partake of the karma because [not clear] what should I do, so why should I take her karma here?

[Maharaj:] You should be very careful, not to take any of our money and use it for your sense gratification.

[Devotee:] But for the food and normal [not clear]

[Maharaj:]You better offer all your food digression [laughter] you should try to convince her, your love for her will be to convince her.

[Devotee:] she doesn’t like, she doesn’t like to be convinced, she is like [not clear 41.57] I wonder what should I do? [not clear] I have to partake of her karma, I must say [not clear] as long as she, she performs [not clear]

[Maharaj:] The integrity of your life before God, is you should support yourself with honest, you should not maintain your body with money that is a product of sin.

[Devotee:] Maharaj, what should I, what should I do, [not clear]…

[Maharaj:] As long as you're in that situation, you are now you should understand, it's very unfortunate and you should become very… very serious about your spiritual life, and pray to God to forgive you, for whatever unavoidable circumstances you're in, but as soon as possible, you should try to create a separate provision for yourself, hmm.

Utilize your time in the service of Krishna

[Devotee:] [Not clear 43.15] If his, whatever you do for Krishna, if it’s by honest, means it's right. But you're taking money from the government, where are you taking?

[Devotee:][not clear 43.34] If I can study hard, if I can study, I can compete with upper class people, not lower class people, if he is a devotee, there is no[not clear] he is a great soul. You should study hard and complete your [not clear] and deserve a post, and then study ahead, what is your comment on that? Why should I take [not clear] as a devotee he has realized that, a spirit soul, [not clear] no caste, nothing.

[Maharaj:] The question, the question is what is he doing with his free time? In other words, if he's engaging in positive spiritual activities, in his reserved time, and he's taking the minimum that he has to be required, to get his education that is also perfect.

[Devotee:] without taking money

[Maharaj:] A time, what is he doing with his time? Sinful or material is, how you're utilizing your time. For instance, if that person is… is let's say, in the medical field, if he's doing medical camps, and he's helping thousands of people become healthier and better in Krishna conscious [audio breaks 45] [audio repeats again]

[Devotee:] [not clear]Suppose, supposed a person has not studied hard, if he had studied hard he can, he can compete with the [not clear] many people with the high class people, not the low class people, he can study hard which is a devotee, [not clear 45.20] study hard and completely [not clear] and then deserve a post, and then study ahead, what is your comment on that? Why should we take a benefit of reserve caste? There is a devotee he has realized that, he is a spirit soul, [not clear] no caste, nothing.

[Maharaj:] The question, the question is what is he doing with his free time? In other words, if he's engaging in positive spiritual activities, in his reserved time, and he's taking the minimum that he has to be required, to get his education that is also perfect.

[Devotee:] without taking money

[Maharaj:] A time, what is he doing with his time? Sinful or material is, how you're utilizing your time. For instance, if that person is… is let's say, in the medical field, if he's doing medical camps, and he's helping thousands of people become healthier and better in Krishna consciousness, and because of that service he just as the absolute bare minimum to get his degrees, that's perfect, because he's utilizing his time for Krishna consciousness. Now if that person is going to cinemas and nightclubs, and in order to enjoy those things, he does the absolute, minimum to get his degree, and that's sinful. If the utilization of the time is for the right purpose, then his overall plan is according to the will of God.

[Devotee:] The basic question is if he would have studied hard then he would have competed with [not clear]…

[Maharaj:]But that is who, that has nothing to do with God, competing with the higher classes, if you have to compete with the higher classes then do it, but if you can utilize your energies more in the service of God and obtain the same result that's better, ah. The question is how your… your utilizing your time, that's how God determines, whether it's good or bad.

[Devotee:] [not clear 47.48]] this candidate studied extremely well, he is also going, he is also doing, [not clear] why is the other person , who has not studied at all, but he is chanting the name of Krishna, and he is doing all here and there, but he is a reserved candidate, supposed to be at reserved place not clear] why should he be given a seat, and if… if he will take the seat is he not sinning?

[Maharaj:] Na, if he's… if he's using his life, his time sinfully, he's using his life for God's service, we can take any facility or anything…

[Devotee:] [not clear] why should he take the medicines if anything like that?

[Maharaj:]The question is what is he utilizing it's time for? If it… if it's for the service of God, it's Krishna conscious, if it's for the service of self it’s sinful., no matter even if that man works like an ass, and earns the competition, that he deserves, if he's not doing it for God, he's a sinful fool, and someone who just does gets by with the least amount, but he's doing it with the proper motivation for God, he's perfect, and if the man works very hard ,and is in the fierce competition and comes out number one, but he does it for God he's… he's perfect. The question is what are your motives, and what are you doing it for? We're not concerned with the mundane concept of whether it's fair or not fair because practically ninety-nine percent of the people who are living fairly, are not doing it for God, they are sinful.

[Devotee:] if that person does it for God, and this person is also doing it for God, but he is not getting it because this person is getting it, what is your answer?

[Maharaj:] But that's not the case. Let's be practical, how many people are doing it for God in this society, huh.

[Devotee:] Another thing is…

[Maharaj:] Answer my question, ah, how many people? This is such a bizarrely theoretically, impractical question, nobody's doing it for God, except a few. So if one person is doing it at the odds are the person who's gonna get it, is not a saintly person, dedicating his life in the service of the Lord, such a person is rare one out of thousands, one out of millions, however he sees fit, he can serve God, whether it's taking the if the convenience of the facilities are working hard, either way, as long as the motive and the purpose is correct, you are perfect.

[Devotee:] And when you become a vaishnava, [not clear] why he has to come down and [not clear]

[Maharaj:] He doesn't have to, but if it… but if it's if it if he can serve God, and serve humanity better by doing that, he should. Don't you think? If a man is dying, and one person just in an emergency situation, just completely unorthodox saves his life, and another person, well I have to go through all the proper, official, legal standards of treating this person, and the person dies. Who's better? The self righteous person is going, [not clear] call the legalistic rules of, or the first one who saves the man's life, no matter what it takes, right? We're not so concerned with all these official college policies, and these ideas of what's fair, and what's not fair. The whole world is practically unfair. Fairness just like we were discussing, the other day, telling the truth in a life of lies, that truth is just part of the lie, right? If you're living a lie, truth within that life, is part of the lie. Is it not? At the same time, if you're living according to the truth, and according to the standard, in order to live according to the truth you have to lie, that lie is part of the truth. Just like one girl was asking, “My parents absolutely will not permit me to chant Hare Krishna, or to meet the devotees?” So the question is, if the only way I can live a Krishna conscious life is to lie to my parents and tell them I'm going to visit somebody, and go to the temple, is that a lie. I told her, “If you're not Krishna conscious, your whole life is a lie.” So if you're telling your parents the truth, it's just truth within a lie, that truth is part of a big lie. The fact is you are the, you are…you are part and parcel of Krishna and your constitutional position is to serve Krishna, and everything outside of that is an illusion, it's a lie. So telling the truth, to just further perpetuate a lie is that the truth? No, but if you are, if she's coming to live a life in devotion, and she has to tell a lie in order to do a live a… live a life of devotion, she's telling a lie, in order to live for truth, whereas the other person is telling the truth, in order to live for a lie. So which is better? So similarly, if everybody's studying and competing, how many people are doing it for God? People are doing it for money and prestige. Ninety-nine percent of the population of these students are doing it for money and prestige, so if one person is really dedicating his life in the service of God, whatever, whichever way, he can best serve God, it's truth, even if it may appear dishonest, by completely mundane standards, he's reaching out and helping people by doing it, that's a higher truth, than just doing everything very honorably, and doing nothing for anyone, except your own senses, huh. So he doesn't have to do that, but if that's the best way, he can serve the Lord, if that's the way, he could use his energies best for the upliftment of humanity he should do it that way. Is that clear?

[Devotee:]In that principle, as the story is there was this devotee sanyasi walking by the road, and one girl was drowning in the water, I mean the river. Is it same, what do you say? [not clear]

A story of two sadhus

[Maharaj:] In that principle, the story is there was two sadhus walking and they came to a river, and there was a young lady, who was unable to cross the river, so very helplessly she asked, “Can you help me cross the river?” So one sadhu picked her up, and they crossed the river, and then he put her down, and then they walked about five kilometer’s, and the other sadhu, he was in so much anxiety, finally he could not control his mind any longer, and he just began to chastise his friend, “How is it possible that you carried a young woman across the river? How is this possible? This is not right, I can't tolerate it” and the sadhu, the first sadhu, looked at him, he said, “The difference between you and me is, I… I put her down five kilometers away, but you've been carrying her in your mind, up until this point.” So we can be very self-righteous, but if our motivation is incorrect, our self-righteousness has no spiritual value, ah. Better, it's better to be to deviate slightly from the official order of the ritual, or the proper way, if your motivation is correct, than to follow everything perfectly.

Jesus showed compassion to the prostitute

That is what Jesus Christ condemned in the Hebrew priests of his time, they were performing all the rituals exactly according to the scripture, but they were doing it for name, fame and prestige and money, huh. Whereas Jesus in order to reach out, and help people, he was breaking one rule after another. He was in, what he was in the house of the Pharisees, one day, the Pharisees were the priests, and this woman came, she was a prostitute, who was not even allowed to step into the house, but she was completely repentant and wanted to just surrender at the feet of Jesus, and take shelter of God. So he told her, she could come in. Now they were completely horrified, “How can you let a low-class, untouchable, won't love women like this in our house?” And then she began to cry, and she began to bathe Jesus’s feet with her tears, and she dried it with her hair, and the Pharisees utterly disgusted by Jesus, they just were criticizing him, all over Jerusalem, all over the town, after they saw this, “How can you allow this to happen?” But Jesus was giving shelter, and made a pure devotee out of this woman, by accepting her repentance, by facilitating, even though he broke some rules, in doing it. Whereas the Pharisees, they could not change anyone's heart, they could not bring anyone closer to God in any way, although they were doing everything perfectly. So who's better, Jesus or the Pharisees? You don't know the name of any of the Pharisees, but everyone knows Jesus. That's the difference. It's a question of our spirit, our heart, not how, not how strictly we're following the rules of the social conduct, and the moral conduct, ah. If you're actually doing it for the betterment of humanity, in the service of God, with devotion, that is perfect, whichever way you choose to do it, as long as that's truly your motive. It's not such a sinful thing. Whereas ninety-nine percent of these students, who are as you say, they're earning the right to be in the, in that particular class of studies, what are they doing it for? Who are they doing it for? They might be doing everything just according to the way it should be done, but it's like so many zeros, it adds up to nothing, because they're not doing it for the soul, according to the goal of life, for the purpose of human life, they're simply doing it for their own aggrandizement. They're like the Pharisees, externally, superficially, they're first-class perfect, but their hearts, are not in the right place.

Service is necessary

It's better to act like a third-class man if your hearts in the right place, right? If that's what's necessary, in your service, ah, of course, it's not absolutely necessary, but it's best to act like a first-class man, with the right, with your heart in the right place. But as a doctor, isn't it sometimes to save a person, you have to put aside all the formalities of what should be done, it shouldn't be done, because it's an emergency, if a doctors not willing to do that, because he's so attached to the proper method of doing things, that means, he has no compassion, he's not concerned with the patient, he's simply concerned with his own, official process of in self-righteousness, and how things must be done, right? A good doctor is a doctor he'll do anything, and everything, to save his patient, even if it means breaking all the rules. Isn't that true? [laughter] Jesus was put into court too. – Haridas Thakur was put into court too, and they were both found guilty, right? Haridas Thakur was taken before the judge, and they…they…they, it was a quick trial, but they charged him, they found him guilty, and they told him, he was to be beaten in twenty-two market places until, he was dead. Jesus Christ they took him before the judge, who happened to be Pontius Pilate, he was the judge, the person who gave the ultimate decisions, in criminal matters at the time, and he ordered him to be killed, to be crucified. Since time immemorial, what to speak of Prahlada Maharaja, Prahlada Maharaja's father sentenced him to death, on so many occasions, but of course, he could not kill him. So it's the nature of the demoniac class, they're always trying to destroy those, who are pure and sacred within society, because there's such a threat. It’s simply religious persecution, that's all. It's just the modern-day, way of persecuting people.

Fight for Krishna

[Devotee:] [not clear 1.1] why is he not competing on his behalf, why is this?

[Maharaj:] Because it's the duty of a devotee as far as possible to defend what belongs to God. Why didn't Arjuna, just say to Krishna, “I'm not going to fight? Why should I retaliate? Why shouldn't I just leave it up to you, and give it to the Kurus?” Ah, because it was Arjuna's duty, as Krishna's to devotee, to pretend, to protect the truth, and protect what belong to God, the whole earth belongs to God. Arjuna was meant to fight for the truth, on behalf of Krishna, fight, so that what belonged to Krishna would be used for Krishna, huh. Your question challenges the very spirit of Bhagavad Gita, Krishna tells Arjuna, and tells all of us through our Arjuna to stand up and fight for Krishna, ah. Whether it's in the courtrooms, or in the battlefield, or on the streets, or within your own mind,[laughter] we all have to fight for Krishna, ah. How many of us, our minds are our best friend, we have so many enemies, in our own mind, practically our whole mind, is our worst enemy, ah. We have to fight for Krishna.

[Devotee:] Also our [not clear 1.3]

[Maharaj:] You can explain the answer.

[Devotee:] in the battlefield of Kurukshetra, why did Arjuna [not clear 1.3.56] same topic,[not clear]

[Maharaj:] How can you question God? First of all you have to ask is Krishna, God. If if you accept Krishna's God, you have to accept he's all good, and therefore whatever he does is for the ultimate good of everyone involved. If he's not God, then you can question him according to these rational logical mundane standards of morality, right? The real question is Krishna, the Absolute Truth? If he is, then whatever he does is perfect. If he isn't, then he's subjected to the same laws, we are. Now according to the Mahabharata, for this book is coming, the book of Mahabharat according to that Scripture, he is the Absolute Truth, he is the Supreme Brahman. He happened to show the universal form to Arjuna, before he told him to kill Karna. So Krishna's decisions are perfect, because he is perfect everything he does is perfect. Karna got liberated. Karna went back to Godhead. I think that was an injustice, if he wasn't killed for all the criminal activities he committed, and all the offenses, he would have gone to hell, but because he was killed in the presence of God, he went back to Godhead. He got the supreme liberation immediately. Do you think he objected to that, maybe while it was taking place, he did, but as soon as it was over with, he was very happy [Laughter] right? Krishna wanted the Pandavas to win, for the sake of all humanity. If the Kurus would have won that war, it would have been a disastrous condition for all humanity, because they were leading the people in irreligion, he wanted the people led toward religion. Now Karna was the only hope, of the Kurus to defeat the Pandavas, because he had the power to kill Arjuna, and without Arjuna the Pandavas could not have won that war. So therefore, Krishna saw, that the benefit of all humanity was more important than one than one little kshatriya code being broken, right?

[Devotee:]But Krishna was the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and he was with Arjuna, could not Arjuna kill Karna by [not clear 1.6] him also?

[Maharaj:] Yes, Krishna wanted to show that a devotee is even too, willing to surrender if his good name and fame of being a good moralist, if it's, if it's to serve the will of God. Sometimes we're very proud of being very moral, and that that pride is also a spiritual obstacle. A devotee should be willing to surrender anything for the service of God, right.

Fix your mind on Krishna

[Devotee:] Even Bhima, Bhima [not clear] in wrong way, [not clear] The T.V. serial that’s going on Mahabharat, the person who is playing the role of Krishna, we are very attached to that person, so wherever , wherever I and many people, whenever we remember , we always remember the face of that person who played Krishna. Is it wrong or is it….

[Maharaj:] Better if you come to Radha Gopinath temple more often, and fix your mind on him, [laughter] it's better to fix your mind on him, then on some movie actress playing maya, [laughter] [Devotee speaks…not clear] So, if he's reminding you of Krishna that's good. But as I say, it's best if you come to the temple and fix your mind on the real Krishna.

As per your karma you carry

[Devotee:] [not clear]

[Maharaj:] It depends on your Karma, your subtle body could carry your mind, intelligence, and ego, to your next body but how it is how, it is carried, is according to your activities and desires, in this life. There are instances in history, someone like Mozart, when he was five years old, he was he was writing beautiful symphonies, nobody even taught him how to do it, how is that possible? Because by his karma, he carried on the intelligence, that he had in his previous life, in the their stories in the Srimad-Bhagavatam of Jadbharat Maharaj, where he was the continuity of his same thinking processes carried on, to three births, ah, so that's very unusual Karma, but according to your karma you can you can, carry on the assets that you achieve in this world, or if you misuse your intelligence, in this, even if you have very very good intelligence, but you misuse it, in this world, if you lose it off, it can be all taken away. You know why.

Do we repeat the cycle of life and death?

[Devotee:] To be extremely honest right now, we repeat the cycle of life and death,

[Maharaj:] Na, the pious life, the most you do is you get prosperity in your next life, until you surrender to God, you have to continue on this cycle of birth and death, until you awaken your love of God, until you go beyond piety and impiety, until you attain the state of pure bhakti, pure devotion, you must keep taking birth, pious pious activities will force you to take another birth, it's according to laws of karma, you have to accept the results of your actions. If you give charity to somebody, you have to come back to receive that charity back from that person. You have no choice, ah. And if you commit, if you commit an offense to a person, you have to come back to accept the result of that offense. If you do good work, you have to come back to reap the results of the good work, if you do bad work you have to come back to me the results of the bad work, but if you engage in God's service, if your life is surrendered in the service of the Lord, that is beyond the good and bad of this world. Bhakti is transcendental, if our activities are based on the principle of bhakti, we never have to take birth again in this material world, because there is no karma in devotional service.

[Devotee:] so what happens to that karma which I already done till now, all the bad karmas that I have done till now what happens to that [not clear]

[Maharaj:] When you, Krishna say’s in Gita,

sarva-dharmān parityajya
mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja
ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo
mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ

[Bg. 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.]

Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender to me. I shall relieve you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear. When you surrender to Krishna, no matter how many sins you have committed, has it makes no difference, Krishna assumes all those sins himself, and cleanses your heart, and cleanses your cleanses you, from all the karmic reaction for whatever you have done, the moment you truly in your heart surrender to Krishna. So I must go. [devotee speaks] Is there any more very important questions, before….

Do we remember this life when we go to Krishna?

[Devotee:] [not clear 1.12.53] whatever you have to leave this body,[not clear] when you go there , you won’t remember anything, you enjoy there in serving Krishna,[not clear] or this is the end here,

[Maharaj:] You can remember anything you want to remember.

[Devotee:] You can,

[Maharaj:] You desire, yes, you can, you are all knowing, the spirit soul is full of knowledge, you can remember any of your experiences in this material world, if that's what you want, but when you're with Krishna, your mind is very happy, the experiences in this world are incomparable, to the joy of the spiritual world. So you're not going to be so anxious to remember all these things. [laughter] But if you can, if you want you can, it is the same soul that is here, that will be there, and that soul is full of knowledge, ah. But in that mood of pure devotion, if it distracts your mind away, from your service to Krishna, you will not want to. But if somebody has helped you in your spiritual life, in this world, you'll easily remember them. Say to Krishna, “Please take help that person come to join us.” [laughter] That's natural, all right if somebody you love very much in this world is still here, you'll say to Krishna, “I love that person very much, let's help him come and be happy with us.” And Krishna will listen, [Devotees laugh] just like devotee oftentimes, oftentimes… oftentimes devotees speak like this amongst each other, that when you go back to Godhead please remember me, and you know tell Krishna that I'm still here.[laughter] It works like that also. So, it's good to make friends which you could devotee with devotees, who are going back to Godhead, you get special connections.

[Devotee:] Maharaj please remember us [laughter]

[Maharaj:] I'll…I’ll try [laughter] but where I'll be, I don't know, if you want me to remember [Laughter]

[Devotee:][not clear]

[Maharaj:] what?

[Devotee:] [not clear]

[Maharaj:] So when will we will see you again, [devotee speaks] today evening, Ha,

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
